extra.message: User input from request.path is passed directly to render_template_string via an f-string, allowing Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI). An attacker can inject Jinja2 template directives in the URL path to execute arbitrary code.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: Hardcoded Flask secret key 'super secret key' is used for session signing. Anyone with access to the source code can forge session cookies, bypass authentication, and tamper with session data.
A static, hardcoded registration token 'a36e990b-0024-4d55-b74a-f8d7528e1764' is seeded into the database. This known token can be used by anyone to bypass registration controls.

Hardcoded admin credentials (admin@evfa.com / admin) and user credentials (user@evfa.com / user) in the database seed file. These well-known credentials grant access to the application in development and potentially production environments.

Untrusted data from the 'preferences' cookie is deserialized with pickle.loads(). An attacker can craft a malicious pickled payload that, when unpickled, executes arbitrary code on the server (Remote Code Execution).

User input from the 'search' query parameter is interpolated directly into a SQL LIKE clause via an f-string inside sqlalchemy.text(). An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL to read, modify, or delete data from the database.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: The /accounts/<user_id>/notes endpoint retrieves notes for any user_id without verifying that the requesting user is authorized to view those notes. An authenticated attacker can enumerate user IDs and access private notes belonging to other users (IDOR).

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: The /account POST route (update_account) is missing the @login_required decorator. This route modifies user account data including password and admin status but can be accessed without authentication, potentially allowing anonymous account manipulation.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: User-supplied form data is used to blindly update the current_user object via __dict__.update(). The AccountForm includes an 'is_admin' field which, combined with the missing @login_required, allows any attacker to self-elevate to administrator privileges.

cwe: CWE-915: Improperly Controlled Modification of Dynamically-Determined Object Attributes

extra.message: User-supplied registration code is interpolated directly into a SQL SELECT query via an f-string inside sqlalchemy.text(). An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL to bypass registration code validation, extract data, or manipulate the database.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: The delete_note endpoint deletes a note by ID without verifying that the note belongs to the authenticated user. Any logged-in user can delete any note in the system by guessing or enumerating note IDs (IDOR).

User-supplied URL from form.url.data is passed directly to urlopen() without validation. An attacker can specify file:// URLs to read arbitrary local files (path traversal), or internal network URLs to perform Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) against internal services.

Note text is rendered with the 'safe' filter (`note.text | safe`), which disables Jinja2 auto-escaping. Since note text is user-supplied (via CKEditor or direct API submission with SQL injection), an attacker can inject arbitrary HTML and JavaScript, leading to stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) for all users viewing the note.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The render_table macro is called with safe_columns=['text'], which renders the 'text' column without HTML escaping. Since note text is user-supplied, this allows stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) for anyone viewing search results.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The render_table macro is called with safe_columns=['text'], which renders the 'text' column without HTML escaping. Since note text is user-supplied, this allows stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) for anyone viewing the personal notes page.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: SQLAlchemy engine is configured with echo=True, which logs every SQL statement to the console. This may expose sensitive data such as password hashes, email addresses, and personal note contents in application logs.

cwe: CWE-532: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File
